content.question: To what extent does early selection in the 6- and 8-year gimnázium system *cause* the high SES-achievement gap and segregation in Hungary, versus acting as a sorting mechanism for inequality that originates elsewhere in the system (residential patterns, preschool quality, family investment)?
content.reduction_path: Conduct quasi-experimental analysis comparing schools/cohorts with and without gimnázium selection pathways, controlling for residential segregation, preschool enrollment, and family background variables. Compare to international systems with similar SES composition but different tracking age. Replicate causal inference using multiple methodologies (difference-in-differences, regression discontinuity at selection thresholds).
